title: This Italian City Is Cracking Down On Overtourism With Restrictions
highlights: surge in tourism in Florence has raised concerns among residents about disruption to their lives and rising  ...    Florence is not the only Italian destination facing the pressures of mass tourism. The Italian city of Florence in Tuscany is introducing new rules to address the growing problem of overtourism. The measures include a ban on key boxes used by short-term rental owners and restrictions on loudspeakers used by tour guides. The key boxes, particularly in the UNESCO-listed centre, have become a source of frustration for residents,  ...    As the city prepares to host G7 tourism ministers, Florence's mayor, Sarah Funaro, introduced a 10-point plan to address these concerns. The entire inner city was designated aWorld Heritage site by UNESCOin 1982, and since then the influx of visitors was exacerbated by low-cost airlines beginning to fly into nearby Pisa in the late 1990s and by high-speed rail services in the 2000s, which made international short-term visits easier and cheaper than ever before.
